AI's Environmental Impact: Rising Emissions, Depleting Water and Vanishing Land

来源: United Nations University Institute for Water, Environment and Health (UNU-INWEH)

发布日期: 2026年6月3日

原文链接: https://unu.edu/inweh/news/environmental-cost-of-AIs-Enrgy-use-carbon-water-and-land-footprints

科技类 环境保护

文章摘要

联合国大学最新研究报告揭示，人工智能数据中心的能源消耗正在对全球自然资源造成巨大压力。到2030年，AI数据中心预计将消耗945太瓦时电力，其水资源消耗将满足撒哈拉以南非洲13亿人的基本用水需求。报告呼吁建立负责任的AI生态系统，平衡技术发展与环境保护。

英文正文

Artificial intelligence is transforming our world, but this technological revolution comes with significant environmental costs that many people have overlooked. A groundbreaking report from the United Nations University Institute for Water, Environment and Health reveals that AI's environmental impact extends far beyond carbon emissions.

By 2030, global data centres powering artificial intelligence are projected to consume 945 terawatt-hours of electricity. This figure is nearly triple the combined annual electricity use of Pakistan, Bangladesh, and Nigeria—countries that together have more than 650 million people. If data centres were treated as a nation, they would become the world's 11th largest electricity consumer.

What surprised researchers most was the hidden water footprint. The water consumption associated with AI data centres will equal the basic annual domestic water needs of all 1.3 billion people in Sub-Saharan Africa. Modern data centres require enormous cooling capacity, and cooling systems consume vast quantities of water. In some regions, expanding compute infrastructure is drawing on water supplies amid prolonged droughts.

The report also highlights a critical misunderstanding about AI sustainability. Most existing assessments focus only on carbon emissions from training large models. However, every kilowatt-hour of electricity used to train or run an AI system also carries a water footprint and a land footprint. These three footprints do not always move in the same direction. Switching from coal to bioenergy can cut carbon emissions by 70 percent, but increase water consumption more than thirty-fold.

Another important finding concerns inference versus training. Public discussion has largely focused on the energy required to train massive models. However, the report reveals that once a model is deployed, inference—the continuous running of models to answer user prompts—becomes the dominant cost, accounting for 80 to 90 percent of total AI energy use. ChatGPT alone processes around 2.5 billion prompts daily, translating to roughly 383 gigawatt-hours of electricity per year.

The report warns of the rebound effect: as models become more efficient, they become cheaper and are used more frequently. Without explicit limits on tokens, resolution, or default output length, improvements at the per-query level are easily wiped out by sheer volume growth. A single short AI-generated video can consume as much electricity as 200,000 spam classifications.

Perhaps most concerning is the geographical concentration of AI infrastructure. Only 32 countries host AI-specialised data centres, and 90 percent of that capacity is concentrated in just two countries. More than 150 countries currently have little or no access to sovereign AI compute. This creates both an economic divide and an environmental justice issue: excluded countries bear the burdens of critical minerals extraction and electronic waste while the strategic benefits flow elsewhere.

Professor Kaveh Madani, Director of UNU-INWEH who led the investigation, emphasized: "This report is not a case against artificial intelligence. It is a call for using it responsibly and addressing its unintended impacts proactively." The report proposes a responsible AI ecosystem built on six principles: transparency, efficiency by design, equity and environmental justice, lifecycle responsibility, global cooperation, and sustainable use.

The message is clear: we have a narrow window to ensure that the backbone of the technological revolution develops within planetary limits. The winners of the AI era will not simply build better models. They will navigate governments, secure resources, protect users, manage risk, and maintain public trust. That challenge may prove far more difficult than building the technology itself.

重点词汇表

单词 音标 词性 释义 例句
consumption /kənˈsʌmpʃən/ n. ★★★★★ 消耗，消费 Energy consumption has increased significantly.
artificial /ˌɑːrtɪˈfɪʃəl/ adj. ★★★★★ 人造的，人工的 Artificial intelligence is changing our lives.
project /prəˈdʒekt/ v. ★★★★★ 预测，预计 Scientists project a rise in temperatures.
infrastructure /ˈɪnfrəstrʌktʃər/ n. ★★★★ 基础设施 The city needs better infrastructure.
capacity /kəˈpæsəti/ n. ★★★★★ 容量，能力 The factory has limited production capacity.
sustainable /səˈsteɪnəbl/ adj. ★★★★ 可持续的 We need sustainable development strategies.
emission /ɪˈmɪʃən/ n. ★★★★ 排放物 Carbon emissions contribute to climate change.
efficient /ɪˈfɪʃənt/ adj. ★★★★★ 高效的 Solar energy is an efficient power source.
deploy /dɪˈplɔɪ/ v. ★★★★ 部署，运用 The company deployed new technology.
domestic /dəˈmestɪk/ adj. ★★★★★ 国内的，家庭的 Domestic water use has increased.
geographical /ˌdʒiəˈɡræfɪkl/ adj. ★★★ 地理的 Geographical factors affect climate.
concentration /ˌkɒnsenˈtreɪʃən/ n. ★★★★ 浓度，集中 There's a high concentration of industries.
responsible /rɪˈspɒnsəbl/ adj. ★★★★★ 负责任的 We must be responsible for our actions.
proactively /prəʊˈæktɪvli/ adv. ★★ 主动地，积极地 Companies should act proactively.
footprint /ˈfʊtprɪnt/ n. ★★ 足迹，痕迹（影响） Our carbon footprint is too large.

长难句解析

1. By 2030, global data centres powering artificial intelligence are projected to consume 945 terawatt-hours of electricity, which is nearly triple the combined annual electricity use of Pakistan, Bangladesh, and Nigeria—countries that together have more than 650 million people.

【语法考点】 非谓语动词（现在分词短语作后置定语）+ 定语从句（which引导）+ 同位语（countries that...）

【解析】 "powering artificial intelligence"是现在分词短语作后置定语，修饰data centres；"which is nearly triple..."是which引导的非限制性定语从句，修饰前面的整个句子内容；"countries that together have more than 650 million people"是同位语，对前面的三个国家进行补充说明。

2. What surprised researchers most was the hidden water footprint that most existing assessments have overlooked.

【语法考点】 主语从句（What引导）+ 定语从句（that引导）

【解析】 "What surprised researchers most"是What引导的主语从句，作整个句子的主语；"that most existing assessments have overlooked"是that引导的定语从句，修饰water footprint。整个句子结构清晰，体现了名词性从句在复杂句子中的重要作用。

3. The report warns that as models become more efficient, they become cheaper and are used more frequently, which means that improvements at the per-query level are easily wiped out by sheer volume growth.

【语法考点】 宾语从句（that引导）+ 时间状语从句（as引导）+ 定语从句（which引导）+ 宾语从句嵌套

【解析】 句子主干为"The report warns that..."，that引导宾语从句；宾语从句内部包含as引导的时间状语从句"as models become more efficient"；"which means that..."是which引导的非限制性定语从句，对前面整个情况进行说明，其中又包含一个that引导的宾语从句。

4. Professor Madani emphasized that this report is not a case against artificial intelligence, but a call for using it responsibly and addressing its unintended impacts proactively.

【语法考点】 宾语从句（that引导）+ 并列结构（not...but...）+ 非谓语动词（动名词using, addressing）

【解析】 句子主干为"Professor Madani emphasized that..."，that引导宾语从句；宾语从句内部使用"not...but..."并列结构，强调"不是...而是..."；"using it responsibly"和"addressing its unintended impacts"都是动名词短语，作为介词for的宾语，体现了动名词在介词后的用法。

高考写作素材积累

【话题关联】科技发展、环境保护、青年责任、可持续发展

精彩表达：

1. come with significant environmental costs — 带来巨大的环境代价

2. extends far beyond — 远不止于

3. account for — 占据；是...的原因

4. wiped out by — 被...抵消

5. geographical concentration — 地理集中

6. environmental justice issue — 环境正义问题

7. narrow window — 短暂的机会窗口

8. within planetary limits — 在地球承受范围内

9. proactive approach — 主动的方式

10. technological revolution — 技术革命

可用句型：

• What surprised researchers most was... (让研究者最惊讶的是...)

• The message is clear: we have a narrow window to... (信息很明确：我们只有短暂的机会来...)

• The winners of the AI era will not simply..., they will... (AI时代的赢家不会仅仅...，他们会...)

• This is not a case against..., but a call for... (这不是反对...的案例，而是呼吁...)

讨论问题

问题1：

According to the report, why is it insufficient to evaluate AI's environmental impact only through carbon emissions?

【中文翻译】 根据报告，为什么仅通过碳排放来评估AI的环境影响是不充分的？

【答题提示】 Consider the three footprints mentioned in the article—carbon, water, and land. Explain how they interact and why focusing on one alone can lead to misleading conclusions. Use the example of switching from coal to bioenergy to illustrate your point.

问题2：

What is the "rebound effect" mentioned in the article, and why does it matter for AI sustainability?

【中文翻译】 文章中提到的"反弹效应"是什么，为什么它对AI的可持续性很重要？

【答题提示】 Define the rebound effect (also called the Jevons Paradox). Explain how efficiency improvements can paradoxically lead to greater overall consumption. Discuss why this matters for AI development and what measures might be needed to address it.

问题3：

The article mentions that AI infrastructure is highly concentrated geographically. What are the implications of this concentration?

【中文翻译】 文章提到AI基础设施在地理上高度集中。这种集中有什么影响？

【答题提示】 Discuss both the economic divide and environmental justice issues. Explain how excluded countries bear burdens (minerals extraction, e-waste) while benefits flow elsewhere. Consider what solutions might promote more equitable AI development globally.

高三英语时文阅读材料 | 每日更新

适合高考阅读理解训练与写作素材积累
